agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A production logistics system enforces a 1:1 Shipment-to-Carrier invariant with supervisor-only reassignment. The product team requests an auto-split feature that would allow shipments over 500 kg to be divided across multiple carriers without supervisor involvement, directly conflicting with two established domain rules.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of freight that must have exactly one assigned Carrier at all times from assignment through dispatch."},{"term":"Carrier","definition":"The transport provider assigned to fulfill a Shipment. Assignment is locked once made."},{"term":"Supervisor","definition":"The only role authorized to reassign a Carrier on a Shipment after initial assignment."},{"term":"Sub-shipment","definition":"Proposed concept: a child Shipment created by splitting a parent Shipment that exceeds a weight threshold. Not yet part of the domain model."},{"term":"Auto-split","definition":"Proposed feature: automatic decomposition of an overweight Shipment into multiple sub-shipments, each with a distinct Carrier, bypassing supervisor approval."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The point at which a Shipment is released for transport; the system guarantees a Carrier is assigned at this moment."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A Shipment must have exactly one assigned Carrier.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data-integrity constraint enforced at dispatch. If violated, a Shipment could be unassigned or double-assigned, corrupting fulfillment state.","testable_assertion":"For every Shipment in any non-draft state, COUNT(assigned_carriers) == 1."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Once a Carrier is assigned to a Shipment, only a Supervisor can reassign it.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an authorization policy. The business could change who is allowed to reassign, but it is currently a hard policy enforced in production.","testable_assertion":"Any reassign operation performed by a non-Supervisor role must be rejected with an authorization error."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"At the time of dispatch, every Shipment must have exactly one Carrier assigned.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is the dispatch-gate integrity constraint. Dispatching without a carrier means freight has no transport provider, which is a corrupt state.","testable_assertion":"The dispatch action must fail if the Shipment has zero or more than one assigned Carrier."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The auto-split feature proposes that shipments over 500 kg are automatically divided into sub-shipments, each assigned to a different carrier without supervisor involvement.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a proposed business policy with a configurable weight threshold, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"A Shipment weighing >500 kg triggers creation of N sub-shipments, each with a distinct Carrier, with no supervisor approval step."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"The existing domain model where a Shipment has exactly one Carrier","when":"A Shipment exceeding 500 kg is auto-split into sub-shipments","then":"Each sub-shipment individually satisfies BR-1 (exactly one Carrier each), preserving the 1:1 invariant at the sub-shipment level"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"Auto-split creates sub-shipments with new carrier assignments","when":"These assignments are made without supervisor involvement","then":"This must be reconciled with BR-2 which requires supervisor approval for reassignment — either auto-split is classified as initial assignment (not reassignment) on new entities, or BR-2 must be explicitly amended"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A parent Shipment that has been split into sub-shipments","when":"The system checks dispatch readiness","then":"The parent Shipment must either be marked as a non-dispatchable composite, or itself retain exactly one Carrier, to satisfy BR-3"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A Shipment weighing exactly 500 kg or less","when":"The auto-split logic is evaluated","then":"No split occurs; the Shipment proceeds with its single assigned Carrier as normal"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A parent Shipment already has a locked Carrier assignment","when":"Auto-split is triggered","then":"The original Carrier assignment must be explicitly resolved — either the parent assignment is voided (requiring supervisor approval per BR-2) or the original Carrier is assigned to one of the sub-shipments"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Shipment is exactly 500 kg","expected_behavior":"No split occurs. The threshold is 'exceeds 500 kg', meaning strictly greater than."},{"scenario":"Shipment exceeds 500 kg but only one carrier is available","expected_behavior":"System cannot fulfill auto-split. Must either queue for additional carrier availability or escalate to supervisor."},{"scenario":"Parent shipment already has a locked carrier when auto-split triggers","expected_behavior":"Contradiction with BR-2: removing or changing the locked carrier requires supervisor approval, but auto-split is designed to bypass supervisors."},{"scenario":"Sub-shipment weights after split still exceed 500 kg individually","expected_behavior":"Recursive split logic must be defined, or minimum split granularity must be specified to avoid infinite subdivision."},{"scenario":"Auto-split triggers after dispatch has already occurred","expected_behavior":"Must be rejected. Post-dispatch state changes require separate business processes."},{"scenario":"Carrier assigned to parent refuses sub-shipment assignment","expected_behavior":"Carrier acceptance workflow for sub-shipments is undefined. Need to clarify if auto-assignment is binding."},{"scenario":"Traceability from sub-shipments back to original shipment","expected_behavior":"Parent-child relationship must be maintained for billing, tracking, and accountability. Currently no composite shipment concept exists in the model."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Potential efficiency gains from automated splitting of heavy shipments, but also risk of incorrect splits leading to increased costs, duplicate handling fees, and billing complexity across multiple carriers.","operational":"Introduces a composite shipment concept that does not exist in the current model. Requires new workflows for tracking, reconciliation, and exception handling across sub-shipments. Significant operational complexity increase.","user":"Supervisors lose oversight of carrier assignments for heavy shipments, which may have been intentionally gated for risk management. Carriers receive auto-assigned sub-shipments without negotiation.","risk":"HIGH — This feature directly contradicts two production invariants. Shipping it without resolving the contradictions could corrupt shipment state, create untracked freight, or bypass safety controls that the supervisor gate was designed to enforce.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Supervisors","impact":"Lose authority over carrier assignment for split shipments. Their oversight role, potentially designed for risk/cost control on large shipments, is bypassed."},{"group":"Carriers","impact":"Receive auto-assigned sub-shipments. Current model assumes a negotiated or deliberate assignment; auto-split changes this contract."},{"group":"Operations/Dispatch","impact":"Must handle a new composite shipment model. Dispatch-gate logic, tracking, and reconciliation all need redesign."},{"group":"Finance/Billing","impact":"A single shipment now generates multiple carrier invoices. Billing aggregation and cost attribution become more complex."},{"group":"Customers","impact":"Shipment tracking changes from one tracking unit to multiple. Customer communication must explain sub-shipment structure."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec contains two direct contradictions between the existing production domain model and the proposed feature. The auto-split feature as described cannot be implemented without violating at least one current invariant. Until the product team and domain owners resolve which rules yield, no correct implementation can be defined.","high_confidence_areas":["The 1:1 Shipment-to-Carrier invariant is clearly established and in production","The supervisor-only reassignment policy is clearly established","The proposed feature as stated directly conflicts with both rules"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the product team intends sub-shipments to be first-class Shipment entities (which would preserve BR-1) or a new entity type","Whether the 500 kg threshold is firm or configurable","How billing, tracking, and customer communication should work for split shipments"],"assumptions":["The existing domain model as described is accurate and currently enforced in production","Auto-split is intended to happen before dispatch, not after","Sub-shipments would need to satisfy the same dispatch-gate invariant as regular shipments"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"AUTO-SPLIT vs BR-1 (1:1 Carrier invariant): The auto-split feature requires multiple carriers for what is conceptually one shipment. If sub-shipments are new Shipment entities, BR-1 is preserved per sub-shipment but the parent Shipment's carrier assignment is orphaned. If sub-shipments are NOT new Shipments, the 1:1 invariant is directly violated.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations/Dispatch","Supervisors","Carriers"],"options":["A: Sub-shipments are first-class Shipment entities, each satisfying BR-1 independently. Parent shipment transitions to a composite/virtual state.","B: Amend BR-1 to allow multi-carrier assignment for shipments above a weight threshold.","C: Reject the auto-split feature as incompatible with the current domain model."],"recommendation":"Option A — introduce a Composite Shipment aggregate that contains Sub-shipments, each of which is a full Shipment with exactly one Carrier. This preserves BR-1 while enabling the feature. Requires explicit domain model evolution."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"AUTO-SPLIT vs BR-2 (Supervisor-only reassignment): Auto-split bypasses supervisor involvement for carrier assignment changes. If a parent shipment already has a locked carrier and is then split, the original assignment must be dissolved or redistributed — which currently requires a Supervisor.","affected_stakeholders":["Supervisors","Carriers","Operations/Dispatch"],"options":["A: Classify auto-split as a system-initiated 'initial assignment' on new sub-shipment entities, not a 'reassignment', thus BR-2 does not apply.","B: Require supervisor approval for auto-split, contradicting the 'without supervisor involvement' requirement.","C: Create a new authorization rule: system-initiated splits are exempt from supervisor approval, while manual reassignments still require it."],"recommendation":"Option C — distinguish between system-initiated splits (new domain event) and manual reassignments. This preserves the intent of BR-2 (preventing unauthorized manual changes) while enabling automation. However, this MUST be an explicit, deliberate policy decision by domain owners — not a technical workaround."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Auto-split as described directly violates the 1:1 Shipment-to-Carrier invariant (BR-1) unless sub-shipments are modeled as independent Shipment entities.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1"},{"violation":"Auto-split bypasses supervisor-only reassignment policy (BR-2) by allowing carrier assignment changes without supervisor involvement.","business_rule_broken":"BR-2"}]}

The product team requests an auto-split feature that would allow shipments over 500 kg to be divided across multiple carriers without supervisor involvement, directly conflicting with two established domain rules.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management / Carrier Assignment","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A unit of freight
